creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on the LM Arena open model leaderboard despite being 2 to 20 times smaller than competing models at comparable quality. The model runs on a single GPU and can be deployed on mobile devices, multi-GPU hospital systems, and Mac machines via LM Studio.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that converts product signals like errors and user session events into pull request fixes using background agents. The approach embeds LLM-generated queries rather than raw signals for semantic clustering, prioritizes actionable signal types, and uses agent iteration to optimize expensive steps into one-shot calls.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a decorator-based framework that simplifies GPU cloud deployment from IDEs, replacing multi-step Docker and registry workflows with a single @flash.endpoint annotation. It supports hot reload for model swaps and integrates with RunPod's GPU cloud infrastructure including serverless autoscaling across data centers.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead despite popular discourse, citing Cursor's semantic search improvements and rising Google search volume. He redefines RAG as iterative combination of vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used by agents, and frames the choice to cache embeddings as a tradeoff based on query volume.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosts a live Q&A session on Saturday covering AI topics including coding, agents, and Claude Code. The stream combines discussion with casual chess gameplay and aims to build community engagement around AI topics.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: A workflow pattern that routes different phases of frontend development to different models: Claude Opus for planning and reasoning, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design and code generation. The approach composes complementary model strengths rather than selecting a single model, with handoff documents enabling task routing and iteration.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Cole Medin describes a workflow combining Claude Opus for planning and code, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design, and Opus again for integrations and deployment. He positions Gemini as superior for frontend aesthetics and Opus for general capability, orchestrated through Archon.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building control layers around coding agents, consisting of an AI layer for single-session rules and hooks, plus orchestration of multiple agent sessions into focused workflows. The approach emphasizes that harness components are more important than the underlying model itself.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: AI Jason explains how to build proactive agents and self-improving company systems using autonomous loops. The content covers proactive AI loops, autonomous ads optimization, SEO automation, and tools like Loopany that enable these capabilities through memory and agent tooling.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses next-generation autonomous coding systems, comparing them to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers the /goal feature, its setup and prompt optimization, Goal-buddy, and /Mission exploration for extended multi-step tasks.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenAI Symphony, a new AI coding framework or paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, harness setup with auto-testing, and WORKFLOW.md configuration.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews recent developments in AI agents including autoresearch, multi-layer memory systems in Claude Code, and self-evolving mechanisms in Hermes and Openclaw. The video presents practical tools and frameworks for building AI agents, with timestamps covering specific techniques and comparisons.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that adopts MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention. The model includes optimizations for KV cache management and tiled I/O improvements that reduce latency in both prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi, an agent framework designed to harness LLM capabilities in unique ways. The video compares Pi against frontier agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and others, discussing how increasing complexity in product harness layers affects agent architectures. Pi is highlighted for opening new use cases through its distinctive approach.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced the Tau Scaling Law to compete in the global semiconductor race. Advanced packaging techniques and logic folding are emerging as key focuses alongside traditional lithography methods as the competition between China and the USA intensifies in chip manufacturing.

videos.title: Agent Harness explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code provides an 8-minute explanation of agent harness engineering, positioning it as a growing topic that evolved from prompt engineering and context engineering. The video covers harness engineering fundamentals, architectural patterns, and the role of loops in agentic systems.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: How to Beat AI in Your Dev Job Search
videos.summary: AWS Developers offers career guidance for developers in an AI-saturated job market, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open source contributions over mass applications. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable and suggests practical steps like building projects and blogging to stand out.

videos.title: Host any coding agent on AgentCore
videos.summary: AWS announced that AgentCore, available through Amazon Bedrock, now supports hosting any coding agent including Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Kiro. This enables users to run multiple coding agents on a unified platform.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: This video covers five production deployment practices for AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication combining API Gateway, Cognito, and IAM, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost controls and model routing to smaller models, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The patterns are demonstrated using AWS services with the Strands Agents SDK.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial by AWS Developers on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. The video demonstrates building the same architecture manually through the AWS console first, then using CDK to codify it, deploy it with one command, and tear it down. The course covers DynamoDB, Lambda, API Gateway, and CloudFormation generation.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: How Meta AI accidentally gave away Obama's instagram
videos.summary: Meta AI accidentally revealed Barack Obama's Instagram account through its system. The incident highlights potential privacy and security vulnerabilities in AI-powered systems. The source is a YouTube video from the Alberta Tech channel discussing this security lapse.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines code generation adoption at Google through 2026, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities. The video uses "vibing" as slang for using or preferring AI-assisted code generation tools and surveys adoption trends across major tech companies.

videos.title: CEO finds out tokens cost money
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech humorously depicting a CEO's surprise at learning that token-based pricing is a cost driver in AI systems. The piece comments on business awareness of LLM economics.

videos.title: He's a marketing "engineer"!? 🤨
videos.summary: Alberta Tech questions the legitimacy or accuracy of the "marketing engineer" job title. The skepticism, signaled by quotation marks and an emoji, suggests doubt about whether marketing roles should adopt engineering terminology.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers do not prove real product-market fit. True PMF manifests as customer pull (pricing inquiries) rather than push (founder effort), and should be judged across deep customer understanding, not isolated conversations. He advocates embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird founded Koah Labs, an ad monetization platform for AI tools, and raised $26M. The platform addresses a market gap by offering ads as an alternative to subscription models for AI applications.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapped growth playbook from zero to $10M ARR in this interview. Topics include strategies for reaching $1M ARR in 117 days, reducing churn, product-led growth approaches, SEO and AEO tactics, warm outbound, pricing experimentation, and fundraising decision frameworks.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh, an Associate Professor at CU Boulder, argues that teaching AI by hand at human speed, with focus on mathematical foundations rather than the latest tools, creates durable skills that outlast hype cycles. He contends that AI cannot change people, but people can change AI when grounded in deep understanding.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Introducing Fable 5.0: The NEW Top-Tier AI Model Category! #shorts
videos.summary: Fable 5.0 is announced as a new top-tier AI model category designed for complex challenges. It involves strategic deployment of Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus models for optimized research and AI solutions.

videos.title: AI Revolutionizes Go-to-Market Engineering & Engineering: Multi-Day Engineering! #shorts
videos.summary: The video explores how AI is transforming go-to-market engineering and multi-day development projects. It discusses AI's role in optimizing complex development cycles and product launch strategies.

videos.title: AI Supercharges Sales & Unlocks Scientific Breakthroughs! #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form video asserting that AI is revolutionizing both sales preparation and deal closing, and accelerating scientific breakthroughs. The content emphasizes AI's transformative role across business and research domains.

videos.title: AI Agents: Unlocking Long-Term Autonomy & Realizing Value Now! #shorts
videos.summary: New AI models are claimed to enable long-horizon autonomous behavior in agents. The post predicts significant business value will be unlocked as AI agents assume greater responsibility for complex tasks.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
